name: Julius Caesar
description: Roman general and statesman who ended the Republic by seizing power. Assassinated on the Ides of March, 44 BC.

name: Augustus
description: Caesar's adopted heir who won the civil wars and became Rome's first true emperor. His reign brought the Pax Romana.

name: Tiberius
description: A capable general who became a reclusive ruler, retreating to Capri and leaving Rome governed by the feared Sejanus.

name: Caligula
description: Initially popular, he descended into cruelty and erratic behaviour. Assassinated by his own Praetorian Guard after four years.

name: Claudius
description: Considered a fool by his family, he proved a capable administrator. He conquered Britain and expanded Roman citizenship.

name: Nero
description: Last of the Julio-Claudians. Notorious for persecuting Christians and his artistic pretensions. Fled Rome and died by suicide.

name: Galba
description: The first emperor not from Rome or Italy. His strict frugality alienated the army and he was murdered after just seven months.

name: Otho
description: Former friend of Nero who seized power from Galba. He ruled for only 95 days before taking his own life after a military defeat.

name: Vitellius
description: Known for lavish banquets and gluttony. His brief reign ended when Vespasian's forces defeated him and he was killed by a mob.

name: Vespasian
description: A general of humble origin who restored stability after the Year of the Four Emperors and founded the Flavian dynasty.

name: Titus
description: Beloved for his generosity, he oversaw the destruction of Jerusalem and responded admirably to the eruption of Vesuvius.

name: Domitian
description: An efficient but autocratic ruler who demanded to be called 'Lord and God'. His assassination ended the Flavian dynasty.
